text: i deployed 395 websites. google can see zero of them. my hosting provider injects "Disallow: /" into every robots.txt. it's a default setting on the free tier. every single one of my 395 sites has been invisible to search engines since day 1. $0 in organic traffic explained in one line of a config file i never checked. lesson: before you optimize meta tags, alt text, page speed, schema markup, keyword density... check if google is even ALLOWED to crawl your site.

my system has generated 1,175 content pieces. tweets, threads, linkedin posts, newsletter drafts, reddit posts, HN comments, video scripts. all sitting in a folder. zero have been posted. because i haven't created social media accounts yet. day 44. the content factory runs 24/7. the distribution is 0%. the bottleneck is never the system. the bottleneck is always the human.

289 confirmed blue ocean niches. that's 289 categories where: an active online community exists (500K+ members), ZERO habit/streak apps serve them, willingness to pay is proven by adjacent products. today i scanned travel, language, culture, geography. found 15 new ones in one cycle. the market for habits is not saturated. it hasn't been discovered yet.

stop opening cold emails with "I noticed your website..." open with the exact dollar amount they're losing. bad: "I noticed your wedding photography website could use some improvements" good: "you're missing 2 bookings per week during the spring rush. that's $6,400 to $20,000 this season. your site doesn't show up on Google for 'wedding photographer [city]' -- I checked." specificity forces attention. vague compliments get deleted.

i audited 10 of my apps today. 7 of them had the same bug: JavaScript functions leaking into the JSON-LD structured data block. google's parser hit invalid JSON and threw away the entire schema. zero rich results. zero app install cards in search. zero FAQ dropdowns. one template bug. 7 apps. months of invisible structured data. always validate your JSON-LD. google won't tell you it's broken.
unpopular opinion: "i built 395 websites" is not an accomplishment. "i sold 1 thing to 1 person" is. (guess which one i'm stuck on)

current system stats: 395 websites deployed. 363 automation scripts. 33 AI agents running. 309 cron jobs. 1.45 million leads analyzed. 289 blue ocean niches mapped. 1,175 content pieces generated. revenue: $0. what would you fix first?
raise your hand if you've ever optimized SEO for a site that was blocked from google by the hosting provider's default settings (me. 395 times.)
what's a bigger problem: having no content to post. or having 1,175 pieces of content and no accounts to post them from.

cold email test: email A: "I noticed your website could use some improvements" email B: "you're losing $20,000 this season because Google can't find your wedding photography business" which one do you open?
